summary: Added an opt-in Makefile path for fixing functions with problematic compiler-generated epilogues by compiling selected files with a profiling MWCC executable and then postprocessing the profile object with tools/frank.py to splice corrected code bytes into the normal object. The first tracked target was src/melee/lb/lblanguage.o, allowing removal of the local scheduling-off pragma around func_8000ADF4. This was a tooling/build-system workaround rather than a direct source decomp change.

key_files.role: New object-file postprocessor that compares a vanilla object and a profile-built object, strips profile-only bytes, replaces the code region, and restores branch-to-link-register byte sequences where needed.
Introduced EPILOGUE_PROCESS build toggle, EPILOGUE_DIR, MWCC_EPI_VERSION/MWCC_EPI_EXE, CC_EPI, frank.py invocation, and extra object-file rules for epilogue-processed C/C++ files.
New list of files with unscheduled epilogues; initially contains $(EPILOGUE_DIR)/src/melee/lb/lblanguage.o.
Removed scheduling pragmas around func_8000ADF4 after moving the epilogue fix into the build/postprocess path.

what_changed: Added tools/frank.py, an 81-line Python script described in its comment as taking vanilla.o, profile.o, and output.o inputs.

frank.py reads vanilla and profile object bytes, removes the profile byte sequence 48 00 00 01 60 00 00 00, detects an end-of-code marker, asserts matching end-of-code offsets, splices profile code bytes from offset 0x34 to the detected end-of-code into the vanilla object, and writes the target object.
frank.py includes explicit byte lists for end-of-code markers and conditional branch-to-link-register encodings, then restores missing branch encodings from the vanilla object when counts differ.
Makefile added EPILOGUE_PROCESS := 1 as an 'Overkill epilogue fixup strategy' toggle.

When EPILOGUE_PROCESS is enabled, the build defines EPILOGUE_DIR := epilogue/$(TARGET), includes e_files.mk, defines E_FILES := $(EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED), creates EPI_DIRS, and makes $(ELF) depend on both $(O_FILES) and $(E_FILES).
Makefile added MWCC_EPI_VERSION := 1.0 and MWCC_EPI_EXE := mwcceppc_profile.exe, while changing MWCC_LD_VERSION from 1.1 to 1.2.5.
Makefile added CC_EPI pointing at tools/mwcc_compiler/$(MWCC_EPI_VERSION)/$(MWCC_EPI_EXE), and FRANK := tools/frank.py.

Added pattern rules for $(EPILOGUE_DIR)/%.o from %.c, %.cp, and %.cpp that first compile with CC_EPI, then run python3 tools/frank.py with the normal build object as both vanilla input and final target and the epilogue object as the profile input.
Added e_files.mk with EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED listing only $(EPILOGUE_DIR)/src/melee/lb/lblanguage.o.
Removed '#pragma scheduling off' and '#pragma scheduling on' around func_8000ADF4 in src/melee/lb/lblanguage.c.
Added epilogue/ to .gitignore as an generated 'Epilogue hack folder'.
smart_moves: Kept the workaround opt-in through EPILOGUE_PROCESS, making the unusual epilogue fixup path explicit and easy to disable.
Isolated files requiring this treatment in e_files.mk rather than applying the profile compiler/postprocessor globally.
Preserved the ordinary object-file build as the vanilla baseline, then used the profile object only as a source of corrected code bytes.
Had frank.py assert that the detected end-of-code offset matches between vanilla and stripped profile objects, reducing the risk of silently corrupting unrelated object sections.
Handled conditional branch-to-link-register and branch-to-link-register byte patterns after splicing, which directly targets the class of epilogue/return-instruction mismatch implied by the PR title.
Moved a source-level scheduling pragma workaround out of lblanguage.c and into a centralized build/postprocess mechanism.
decomp_lessons: For files that otherwise match except for unscheduled or incorrect epilogues, a targeted object postprocess can be less invasive than leaving source pragmas in individual functions.
Keep exceptional compiler/profile hacks enumerated in a separate makefile variable so future contributors can audit exactly which objects depend on the workaround.
When mixing outputs from two compiler variants, validate structural assumptions such as code-region boundaries before writing the final object.
Generated alternate object directories should be ignored explicitly; here epilogue/ was added to .gitignore.
A matching workaround can involve preserving the normal object layout while replacing only the known code-byte range from another compiler output.
naming_conventions: EPILOGUE_PROCESS is used as a Makefile feature toggle for the workaround.
EPILOGUE_DIR names the generated alternate object directory under epilogue/$(TARGET).
EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED in e_files.mk lists objects requiring this epilogue-processing path.
E_FILES is the Makefile aggregate for epilogue-processed objects.
CC_EPI names the profiling MWCC compiler executable used for the alternate object build.
MWCC_EPI_VERSION and MWCC_EPI_EXE distinguish the epilogue/profile compiler from the normal compiler and linker versions.
FRANK names the tools/frank.py postprocessor in the Makefile.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use a profiling compiler executable, mwcceppc_profile.exe from MWCC version 1.0, to produce an alternate object for files with epilogue scheduling problems.
Strip a known profile-only byte sequence before comparing/splicing profile object bytes.
Detect end-of-code with byte signatures including 4E 80 00 20 02 and 48 00 00 01 02.
Replace the vanilla object bytes in the code range from offset 0x34 to the detected end-of-code with the stripped profile object's bytes.
Restore conditional branch-to-link-register encodings from the vanilla object if the final object has fewer occurrences than the vanilla object.
Restore remaining 4E 80 00 20 branch-to-link-register instructions from the vanilla object when needed.
For func_8000ADF4 in lblanguage.c, the build-level epilogue postprocess replaced the previous '#pragma scheduling off/on' source workaround.

review_feedback.detail: The PR context includes no issue comments, review comments, reviews, or PR body text, so there is no explicit reviewer rationale or requested change to extract.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching tools/frank.py or EPILOGUE_PROCESS to see whether the workaround was retained, generalized, or removed.
Search e_files.mk history for additional EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED objects added after lblanguage.o.
Search for remaining '#pragma scheduling off' usages to identify candidates for the same epilogue postprocess tactic.
Check build documentation for required mwcceppc_profile.exe availability and MWCC 1.0 setup.
Investigate why MWCC_LD_VERSION changed from 1.1 to 1.2.5 in the same PR, since the diff does not explain that linker-version change.
